program_name: Languages of Asia and North Africa for Business and International Cooperation - South East Asia
teaching_language: English track within a broader Italian/English degree [uncertain]
degree_class: LM-38 R (Modern languages for communication and international cooperation)

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need at least a three-year bachelor's degree. For the South East Asia curriculum, the apply.unive.it page requires 42 ECTS in coherent prior studies, including 36 ECTS in Chinese and South Asian languages and literatures focused on Thailand and 6 ECTS in area, language, legal, social-science, or history subjects; the Teaching Committee evaluates the transcript, CV, and motivation letter.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent first-cycle university qualification.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: For the South East Asia curriculum, applicants must have at least 42 ECTS: 36 ECTS in Chinese and South Asian languages and literatures (Thailand on the apply.unive.it page), plus 6 ECTS in one or more of the following areas: geography, law, glottology and linguistics, didactics of modern languages, East Asian archaeology/art/philosophy, East and South-East Asian history, anthropology, economics, political science, sociology, or Asian/African history. If curricular requirements are missing, they must be completed before enrolment through bachelor's-level single courses.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: The programme page says applicants with an L-11 or L-12 bachelor's degree, or an equivalent degree, and a minimum final grade of 90/110 are generally considered adequately prepared. Country-specific validity rules for international qualifications also apply. No separate minimum CGPA was found on the South East Asia apply.unive.it page.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: International applicants first undergo the Welcome Unit's preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it. The programme's Teaching Committee then evaluates the undergraduate transcript, curriculum vitae, and motivation letter to assess curricular coherence and personal preparation, and it may require an additional interview.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certification on Ca' Foscari's official list.
A certificate from the CLA centre or from a university language school included in the recognised list.

Electronic upload of the certificate during enrolment; the South East Asia apply.unive.it page states that applicants may apply before obtaining the certificate but must provide it to complete enrolment.
exemption_rules: At least one year of higher education in a country where English is the medium of instruction.
A secondary-school or university qualification earned through a study path taught entirely in English.
At least one full secondary-school exchange year at an institution where English was the language of instruction.
Specified Italian degree classes in languages or language mediation with a three-year English study path.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: The programme page requires B2 English by the enrolment deadline. The general Master's Degree Programmes page lists recognised proofs and exemptions, while the South East Asia apply.unive.it page says applicants may apply without the certificate but must upload it to complete enrolment.

rounds: For 2026/2027 international applications, Ca' Foscari states that applications begin on 17 December 2025 for applicants not requiring a study visa; exact citizenship-based round end dates are not reproduced in the official article body consulted [uncertain].
On the programme admission page, entry-requirements evaluation sessions run from 7 April to 4 June 2026, from 6 July to 20 August 2026, and from 1 October to 17 November 2026.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: 30 September 2026; additional enrolment period from 1 October to 18 December 2026 with a EUR 50 late fee.
application_deadline_eu.notes: Non-EU citizens already resident in Italy with a residence permit valid for university enrolment are directed to the EU/equivalent route. The programme page provides 2026 entry-evaluation and enrolment dates, while the central apply.unive.it article says no-study-visa applicants must follow citizenship-based admission rounds [uncertain].

rounds: For 2026/2027 international applications, Ca' Foscari states that applications begin on 17 December 2025 for BA/MA applicants requiring a study visa; exact citizenship-based round deadlines are not reproduced in the accessible official article body [uncertain].
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Accepted applicants requiring a visa must submit Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 September 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Exact 2026/2027 round availability depends on the citizenship-based apply.unive.it table and is not stated in the official article body consulted [uncertain].
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Visa applicants must first complete the pre-evaluation on apply.unive.it, then use the eligibility letter to complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and the study-visa procedure. Ca' Foscari states that later Universitaly submissions will not be considered.

required_documents.notes: European applicants may use an ID card; proof of renewal or first request may be accepted where applicable.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or renewal/first-request receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records for all bachelor's degree years, with official English or Italian translation if needed
required_documents.notes: If the applicant is still studying, a provisional transcript is still mandatory.
Listed as highly recommended for master's applications.
Mandatory for master's applications and used by the Teaching Committee to assess personal preparation.
The South East Asia curriculum requires a dedicated motivation letter on apply.unive.it.
The programme admission page lists possible exam syllabi among useful documents for the entry-requirements evaluation process.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma, with official English or Italian translation if needed
required_documents.notes: A provisional document can be used during application, but the final diploma must be uploaded before the end of the enrolment period.

required_documents.document_name: Statement of Comparability by CIMEA, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondence with CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement from an EHEA institution
required_documents.notes: Ca' Foscari strongly recommends obtaining one of these before applying; one of them is required to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: English B2 certificate or exemption evidence
required_documents.notes: The South East Asia curriculum requires B2 English for enrolment; the apply.unive.it page says applicants may apply without the certificate but must provide it to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and Italian study visa
required_documents.notes: Applicants who require a visa must complete Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 September 2026 and then upload visa-related evidence in the relevant apply.unive.it task.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard entry test; possible Teaching Committee interview
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Open-access admission to the South East Asia curriculum. The Teaching Committee may require an additional interview if personal preparation is not automatically considered adequate.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed test dates were published for the standard open-access route; any additional interview is arranged case by case after academic evaluation.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: There is no standard written entry-test threshold. Applicants with an L-11 or L-12 degree, or equivalent, and a minimum final grade of 90/110 are generally considered adequately prepared; other applicants may be reviewed more closely and interviewed.

source_quotes.quote: Languages of Asia and North Africa for Business and International Cooperation - South East Asia
must hold at least a three-year bachelor's degree
A minimum B2-level knowledge of English is mandatory for enrolment
deadline for sending the application on Universitaly for 2026/2027 academic year has been set on September 30th, 2026

uncertainty_notes: The 2026/2027 International programmes list names the English-track entry 'South East Asia', and the apply.unive.it curriculum page says 'Study language English', but the degree home page says 'Italian/English' and the broader admission page describes the master's degree programme as taught in Italian.
The degree home page still shows 'Enrolment from 2025/2026' even though the official international-programmes list includes this entry for 2026/2027.

The central apply.unive.it 2026/2027 admission-rounds article links citizenship-based round tables hosted on Google Drive. Because the project allows only unive.it and apply.unive.it sources, exact round closing dates were not transcribed from those external files.
